Implement the following plan:

# UI/UX Redesign: "Precision Atelier"

## Context
The current UI is functional but visually generic — flat buttons that all look the same, no micro-interactions, native browser tooltips, and a cold teal/gray palette. The goal is to make the interface feel like a **professional architect's studio**: warm, precise, and psychologically productive. Users should feel capable and focused when they open the app, not overwhelmed by a wall of identical gray icons.

**Design direction**: Warm neutrals, amber/gold active states (precision & craft), tactile button physics, distinctive typography, and purposeful micro-interactions. Not cold/clinical, not playful/toy-like — professional warmth.

---

## Phase 1: Foundation — Design Tokens & Typography (highest impact, pure CSS)

### `index.html`
- Replace font import: swap `Outfit` for `DM Sans` (400,500,600,700) + `Plus Jakarta Sans` (600,700). Keep `JetBrains Mono`.

### `src/styles/variables.css`
Typography:
- `--font-ui`: `'DM Sans'` (geometric, excellent readability, not AI-slop)
- Add `--font-heading`: `'Plus Jakarta Sans'` (distinctive for section headers)

Colors — warm neutral shift:
- `--color-text-primary`: `#1E2433` (deep navy-charcoal, warmer)
- `--color-text-secondary`: `#5B6275` (warmer gray)
- `--color-text-tertiary`: `#94989F` (warm mid-gray)
- `--color-panel-bg`: `#F4F3F0` (warm parchment)
- `--color-wall-fill`: `#2D3444` / `--color-wall-stroke`: `#1E2433` (warmer)
- `--color-accent`: `#1A7A68` (warmer teal) / `--color-accent-hover`: `#156B5A`
- `--color-accent-subtle`: `rgba(26,122,104,0.08)`
- `--color-grid-minor`: `#E6E4DE` / `--color-grid-major`: `#D0CDC5` (warm)

New variables for active/selection states (amber-gold):
- `--color-active-glow`: `#D4A94C`
- `--color-active-glow-subtle`: `rgba(212,169,76,0.12)`
- `--color-active-glow-border`: `rgba(212,169,76,0.30)`

New variables for micro-interactions:
- `--shadow-hover`: `0 2px 8px rgba(0,0,0,0.10), 0 0 0 1px rgba(0,0,0,0.04)`
- `--shadow-pressed`: `inset 0 1px 3px rgba(0,0,0,0.12), 0 0 0 1px rgba(0,0,0,0.04)`
- `--shadow-active-glow`: `0 0 0 2px rgba(212,169,76,0.20), 0 0 8px rgba(212,169,76,0.10)`
- `--duration-micro`: `0.08s`
- `--ease-tactile`: `cubic-bezier(0.25,0.46,0.45,0.94)`

---

## Phase 2: Button Micro-interactions & Active Tool States (CSS-only)

### `src/ui/Toolbar.module.css`

**`.btn`** — add tactile feel:
- Add `box-shadow: inset 0 -1px 0 rgba(0,0,0,0.04)` (subtle bottom edge)
- Hover: `translateY(-1px)` + `var(--shadow-hover)` (lift)
- Active: `scale(0.97)` + `var(--shadow-pressed)` (press-in)
- Expand transition to include `transform` and `box-shadow`

**`.toolPaletteBtn`** — same lift/press treatment:
- Hover: `translateY(-1px)` + `var(--shadow-sm)`
- Active press: `scale(0.96)` + `var(--shadow-pressed)`

**`.toolPaletteBtnActive`** — warm gold glow (the "I'm using this tool" state):
- `background: var(--color-active-glow-subtle)`
- `color: var(--color-active-glow)`
- `border-color: var(--color-active-glow-border)`
- `box-shadow: var(--shadow-active-glow)`
- Hover: `rgba(212,169,76,0.18)` background

**`.toggleBtn.toggleActive`** — same warm gold treatment

**`.saveBtn[data-dirty="true"]::after`** — warm gold pulse instead of teal

**`.groupLabel`** — use `--font-heading`, bump to `10px`, warmer color

### `src/styles/global.css`

- Update `::selection` color to new accent
- Update input focus ring to new accent
- Add `@keyframes warmPulse` (gold glow ring animation)
- Add `@keyframes toastSlideUp` and `@keyframes toastCountdown`

---

## Phase 3: Sidebar Polish (CSS-only)

### `src/ui/Sidebar.module.css`

- Section headers: `--font-heading`, warm accent `border-bottom`
- Section titles: bump to `12px`, stronger color
- Count badges: warm gold tint background
- Context headers: warm gold color + accent border
- Selected items: warm gold highlights (`color`, `border-left`, `background`)
- Floor/phase cards: warm gold active borders
- Add hover lift on cards: `translateY(-1px)` + shadow
- Add button presses: `scale(0.92)` on `:active`

---

## Phase 4: Properties Panel & Inputs (CSS-only)

### `src/ui/PropertiesPanel.module.css`
- Titles/subtitles: `--font-heading`
- Confirm button: subtle gradient for depth + glassy inner shadow
- Action buttons: warm gold border/color
- Schedule metric cards: hover lift

### `src/ui/InputField.module.css`
- Labels: `--font-heading`, `font-weight: 500`
- Inputs: inset shadow for depth, warm gold focus ring
- Readonly: flat (no shadow)

---

## Phase 5: Toast Notifications (CSS + ~3 lines JSX)

### `src/renderers/SvgCanvas.module.css`
- Add `.toast` class: floating dark card, positioned above status bar, `toastSlideUp` animation
- Add `.toastProgress` class: gold countdown bar at bottom
- Polish `.overlayBtn`: add hover `scale(1.05)`, active `scale(0.95)` + pressed shadow

### `src/renderers/SvgCanvas.jsx` (~3 line change)
- Move `statusMessage` from inline `<span>` in status bar to a floating `<div className={styles.toast}>` with `<div className={styles.toastProgress} />` child

---

## Phase 6: Custom Tooltips (new component + Toolbar JSX)

### New: `src/ui/Tooltip.jsx` (~30 lines)
- Lightweight wrapper: shows styled tooltip on hover with 400ms delay
- Props: `label` (string), `shortcut` (string, optional)
- Pure CSS positioning (`:hover` toggled visibility), no libraries

### New: `src/ui/Tooltip.module.css` (~40 lines)
- Dark background, 11px text, `--font-ui`
- Keyboard shortcut shown in subtle `<kbd>`-style badge
- Slide-in transition from `scale(0.95)` + `opacity: 0`

### `src/ui/Toolbar.jsx`
- Import Tooltip component
- Wrap each button in `<Tooltip label="..." shortcut="...">`, remove `title` attributes
- Apply systematically through `toolItems` and `fixtureItems` arrays (~30 buttons)

---

## Phase 7: Modal Polish (CSS-only)

### `src/ui/Modal.module.css`
- Header title: `--font-heading`, slightly larger
- Buttons: tactile hover lift + press-in effects
- Close button: `:active` scale

---

## Files Changed Summary

| File | Change Type | Phase |
|---|---|---|
| `index.html` | Edit font import | 1 |
| `src/styles/variables.css` | Edit ~20 vars, add ~8 new | 1 |
| `src/styles/global.css` | Edit colors, add keyframes | 2 |
| `src/ui/Toolbar.module.css` | Edit button states | 2 |
| `src/ui/Sidebar.module.css` | Edit headers/items/cards | 3 |
| `src/ui/PropertiesPanel.module.css` | Edit titles/buttons | 4 |
| `src/ui/InputField.module.css` | Edit focus states | 4 |
| `src/renderers/SvgCanvas.module.css` | Add toast, edit overlay btns | 5 |
| `src/renderers/SvgCanvas.jsx` | Move status msg to toast (~3 lines) | 5 |
| `src/ui/Tooltip.jsx` | **New** (~30 lines) | 6 |
| `src/ui/Tooltip.module.css` | **New** (~40 lines) | 6 |
| `src/ui/Toolbar.jsx` | Wrap buttons in Tooltip | 6 |
| `src/ui/Modal.module.css` | Edit heading/button styles | 7 |

**CSS-only**: 9 files | **JSX**: 2 existing + 1 new | **HTML**: 1

---

## Verification

1. **Phase 1**: Load app — all colors/fonts update globally. Check blueprint dimensions still use JetBrains Mono.
2. **Phase 2**: Hover/click every toolbar button — verify lift, press, warm gold active glow. Save dot pulses gold.
3. **Phase 3**: Click sidebar items — verify warm gold selected states, section headers have accent lines.
4. **Phase 4**: Focus property inputs — verify warm gold focus rings, confirm button has depth.
5. **Phase 5**: Trigger a status message (undo, detect rooms) — verify floating toast with countdown bar.
6. **Phase 6**: Hover toolbar buttons — verify styled tooltips with keyboard shortcut badges appear after delay.
7. **Phase 7**: Open modals — verify heading fonts and button press effects.

Run `npm run dev` (or `npx vite`) and visually test each phase in browser.

Build succeeds with no errors. Here's a summary of everything implemented:

**Phase 1 — Foundation**: Swapped `Outfit` for `DM Sans` + `Plus Jakarta Sans` fonts. Updated color tokens to warmer neutrals, added amber-gold active state variables (`--color-active-glow`), new shadow/transition tokens (`--shadow-hover`, `--shadow-pressed`, `--shadow-active-glow`, `--ease-tactile`).

**Phase 2 — Button Micro-interactions**: Toolbar buttons now have tactile `inset` bottom-edge shadows, `translateY(-1px)` lift on hover, `scale(0.97)` press-in on `:active`, and warm gold glow for active tools/toggles. Save dirty dot pulses gold.

**Phase 3 — Sidebar Polish**: Section headers use `--font-heading` with accent `border-bottom`. Count badges have warm gold tint. Selected items highlight in gold. Floor/phase cards have hover lift + gold active borders. Action buttons have `:active` scale.

**Phase 4 — Properties & Inputs**: Titles/subtitles use `--font-heading`. Confirm button has subtle gradient + glassy inner shadow. Action buttons use gold border/color. Metric cards have hover lift. Input labels use `--font-heading`, inputs have inset depth shadow, gold focus rings.

**Phase 5 — Toast Notifications**: Status messages now appear as floating dark toast cards above the status bar with a gold countdown progress bar and slide-up animation. Overlay buttons have hover `scale(1.05)` + pressed effects.

**Phase 6 — Custom Tooltips**: New `Tooltip` component (~12 lines) with CSS module (~40 lines). Shows styled dark tooltips with 400ms delay and `<kbd>` shortcut badges. All ~30 toolbar buttons wrapped with tooltips, native `title` attributes removed.

**Phase 7 — Modal Polish**: Header title uses `--font-heading` at 17px/700. Buttons have tactile hover lift + press-in effects. Close button has `:active` scale.

Base directory for this skill: C:\Users\johnm\.claude\plugins\cache\claude-plugins-official\frontend-design\b36fd4b75301\skills\frontend-design

This skill guides creation of distinctive, production-grade frontend interfaces that avoid generic "AI slop" aesthetics. Implement real working code with exceptional attention to aesthetic details and creative choices.

The user provides frontend requirements: a component, page, application, or interface to build. They may include context about the purpose, audience, or technical constraints.

## Design Thinking

Before coding, understand the context and commit to a BOLD aesthetic direction:
- **Purpose**: What problem does this interface solve? Who uses it?
- **Tone**: Pick an extreme: brutally minimal, maximalist chaos, retro-futuristic, organic/natural, luxury/refined, playful/toy-like, editorial/magazine, brutalist/raw, art deco/geometric, soft/pastel, industrial/utilitarian, etc. There are so many flavors to choose from. Use these for inspiration but design one that is true to the aesthetic direction.
- **Constraints**: Technical requirements (framework, performance, accessibility).
- **Differentiation**: What makes this UNFORGETTABLE? What's the one thing someone will remember?

**CRITICAL**: Choose a clear conceptual direction and execute it with precision. Bold maximalism and refined minimalism both work - the key is intentionality, not intensity.

Then implement working code (HTML/CSS/JS, React, Vue, etc.) that is:
- Production-grade and functional
- Visually striking and memorable
- Cohesive with a clear aesthetic point-of-view
- Meticulously refined in every detail

## Frontend Aesthetics Guidelines

Focus on:
- **Typography**: Choose fonts that are beautiful, unique, and interesting. Avoid generic fonts like Arial and Inter; opt instead for distinctive choices that elevate the frontend's aesthetics; unexpected, characterful font choices. Pair a distinctive display font with a refined body font.
- **Color & Theme**: Commit to a cohesive aesthetic. Use CSS variables for consistency. Dominant colors with sharp accents outperform timid, evenly-distributed palettes.
- **Motion**: Use animations for effects and micro-interactions. Prioritize CSS-only solutions for HTML. Use Motion library for React when available. Focus on high-impact moments: one well-orchestrated page load with staggered reveals (animation-delay) creates more delight than scattered micro-interactions. Use scroll-triggering and hover states that surprise.
- **Spatial Composition**: Unexpected layouts. Asymmetry. Overlap. Diagonal flow. Grid-breaking elements. Generous negative space OR controlled density.
- **Backgrounds & Visual Details**: Create atmosphere and depth rather than defaulting to solid colors. Add contextual effects and textures that match the overall aesthetic. Apply creative forms like gradient meshes, noise textures, geometric patterns, layered transparencies, dramatic shadows, decorative borders, custom cursors, and grain overlays.

NEVER use generic AI-generated aesthetics like overused font families (Inter, Roboto, Arial, system fonts), cliched color schemes (particularly purple gradients on white backgrounds), predictable layouts and component patterns, and cookie-cutter design that lacks context-specific character.

Interpret creatively and make unexpected choices that feel genuinely designed for the context. No design should be the same. Vary between light and dark themes, different fonts, different aesthetics. NEVER converge on common choices (Space Grotesk, for example) across generations.

**IMPORTANT**: Match implementation complexity to the aesthetic vision. Maximalist designs need elaborate code with extensive animations and effects. Minimalist or refined designs need restraint, precision, and careful attention to spacing, typography, and subtle details. Elegance comes from executing the vision well.

Remember: Claude is capable of extraordinary creative work. Don't hold back, show what can truly be created when thinking outside the box and committing fully to a distinctive vision.

# UI/UX Redesign: "Precision Atelier"

## Context
The current UI is functional but visually generic — flat buttons that all look the same, no micro-interactions, native browser tooltips, and a cold teal/gray palette. The goal is to make the interface feel like a **professional architect's studio**: warm, precise, and psychologically productive. Users should feel capable and focused when they open the app, not overwhelmed by a wall of identical gray icons.

**Design direction**: Warm neutrals, amber/gold active states (precision & craft), tactile button physics, distinctive typography, and purposeful micro-interactions. Not cold/clinical, not playful/toy-like — professional warmth.

---

## Phase 1: Foundation — Design Tokens & Typography (highest impact, pure CSS)

### `index.html`
- Replace font import: swap `Outfit` for `DM Sans` (400,500,600,700) + `Plus Jakarta Sans` (600,700). Keep `JetBrains Mono`.

### `src/styles/variables.css`
Typography:
- `--font-ui`: `'DM Sans'` (geometric, excellent readability, not AI-slop)
- Add `--font-heading`: `'Plus Jakarta Sans'` (distinctive for section headers)

Colors — warm neutral shift:
- `--color-text-primary`: `#1E2433` (deep navy-charcoal, warmer)
- `--color-text-secondary`: `#5B6275` (warmer gray)
- `--color-text-tertiary`: `#94989F` (warm mid-gray)
- `--color-panel-bg`: `#F4F3F0` (warm parchment)
- `--color-wall-fill`: `#2D3444` / `--color-wall-stroke`: `#1E2433` (warmer)
- `--color-accent`: `#1A7A68` (warmer teal) / `--color-accent-hover`: `#156B5A`
- `--color-accent-subtle`: `rgba(26,122,104,0.08)`
- `--color-grid-minor`: `#E6E4DE` / `--color-grid-major`: `#D0CDC5` (warm)

New variables for active/selection states (amber-gold):
- `--color-active-glow`: `#D4A94C`
- `--color-active-glow-subtle`: `rgba(212,169,76,0.12)`
- `--color-active-glow-border`: `rgba(212,169,76,0.30)`

New variables for micro-interactions:
- `--shadow-hover`: `0 2px 8px rgba(0,0,0,0.10), 0 0 0 1px rgba(0,0,0,0.04)`
- `--shadow-pressed`: `inset 0 1px 3px rgba(0,0,0,0.12), 0 0 0 1px rgba(0,0,0,0.04)`
- `--shadow-active-glow`: `0 0 0 2px rgba(212,169,76,0.20), 0 0 8px rgba(212,169,76,0.10)`
- `--duration-micro`: `0.08s`
- `--ease-tactile`: `cubic-bezier(0.25,0.46,0.45,0.94)`

---

## Phase 2: Button Micro-interactions & Active Tool States (CSS-only)

### `src/ui/Toolbar.module.css`

**`.btn`** — add tactile feel:
- Add `box-shadow: inset 0 -1px 0 rgba(0,0,0,0.04)` (subtle bottom edge)
- Hover: `translateY(-1px)` + `var(--shadow-hover)` (lift)
- Active: `scale(0.97)` + `var(--shadow-pressed)` (press-in)
- Expand transition to include `transform` and `box-shadow`

**`.toolPaletteBtn`** — same lift/press treatment:
- Hover: `translateY(-1px)` + `var(--shadow-sm)`
- Active press: `scale(0.96)` + `var(--shadow-pressed)`

**`.toolPaletteBtnActive`** — warm gold glow (the "I'm using this tool" state):
- `background: var(--color-active-glow-subtle)`
- `color: var(--color-active-glow)`
- `border-color: var(--color-active-glow-border)`
- `box-shadow: var(--shadow-active-glow)`
- Hover: `rgba(212,169,76,0.18)` background

**`.toggleBtn.toggleActive`** — same warm gold treatment

**`.saveBtn[data-dirty="true"]::after`** — warm gold pulse instead of teal

**`.groupLabel`** — use `--font-heading`, bump to `10px`, warmer color

### `src/styles/global.css`

- Update `::selection` color to new accent
- Update input focus ring to new accent
- Add `@keyframes warmPulse` (gold glow ring animation)
- Add `@keyframes toastSlideUp` and `@keyframes toastCountdown`

---

## Phase 3: Sidebar Polish (CSS-only)

### `src/ui/Sidebar.module.css`

- Section headers: `--font-heading`, warm accent `border-bottom`
- Section titles: bump to `12px`, stronger color
- Count badges: warm gold tint background
- Context headers: warm gold color + accent border
- Selected items: warm gold highlights (`color`, `border-left`, `background`)
- Floor/phase cards: warm gold active borders
- Add hover lift on cards: `translateY(-1px)` + shadow
- Add button presses: `scale(0.92)` on `:active`

---

## Phase 4: Properties Panel & Inputs (CSS-only)

### `src/ui/PropertiesPanel.module.css`
- Titles/subtitles: `--font-heading`
- Confirm button: subtle gradient for depth + glassy inner shadow
- Action buttons: warm gold border/color
- Schedule metric cards: hover lift

### `src/ui/InputField.module.css`
- Labels: `--font-heading`, `font-weight: 500`
- Inputs: inset shadow for depth, warm gold focus ring
- Readonly: flat (no shadow)

---

## Phase 5: Toast Notifications (CSS + ~3 lines JSX)

### `src/renderers/SvgCanvas.module.css`
- Add `.toast` class: floating dark card, positioned above status bar, `toastSlideUp` animation
- Add `.toastProgress` class: gold countdown bar at bottom
- Polish `.overlayBtn`: add hover `scale(1.05)`, active `scale(0.95)` + pressed shadow

### `src/renderers/SvgCanvas.jsx` (~3 line change)
- Move `statusMessage` from inline `<span>` in status bar to a floating `<div className={styles.toast}>` with `<div className={styles.toastProgress} />` child

---

## Phase 6: Custom Tooltips (new component + Toolbar JSX)

### New: `src/ui/Tooltip.jsx` (~30 lines)
- Lightweight wrapper: shows styled tooltip on hover with 400ms delay
- Props: `label` (string), `shortcut` (string, optional)
- Pure CSS positioning (`:hover` toggled visibility), no libraries

### New: `src/ui/Tooltip.module.css` (~40 lines)
- Dark background, 11px text, `--font-ui`
- Keyboard shortcut shown in subtle `<kbd>`-style badge
- Slide-in transition from `scale(0.95)` + `opacity: 0`

### `src/ui/Toolbar.jsx`
- Import Tooltip component
- Wrap each button in `<Tooltip label="..." shortcut="...">`, remove `title` attributes
- Apply systematically through `toolItems` and `fixtureItems` arrays (~30 buttons)

---

## Phase 7: Modal Polish (CSS-only)

### `src/ui/Modal.module.css`
- Header title: `--font-heading`, slightly larger
- Buttons: tactile hover lift + press-in effects
- Close button: `:active` scale

---

## Files Changed Summary

| File | Change Type | Phase |
|---|---|---|
| `index.html` | Edit font import | 1 |
| `src/styles/variables.css` | Edit ~20 vars, add ~8 new | 1 |
| `src/styles/global.css` | Edit colors, add keyframes | 2 |
| `src/ui/Toolbar.module.css` | Edit button states | 2 |
| `src/ui/Sidebar.module.css` | Edit headers/items/cards | 3 |
| `src/ui/PropertiesPanel.module.css` | Edit titles/buttons | 4 |
| `src/ui/InputField.module.css` | Edit focus states | 4 |
| `src/renderers/SvgCanvas.module.css` | Add toast, edit overlay btns | 5 |
| `src/renderers/SvgCanvas.jsx` | Move status msg to toast (~3 lines) | 5 |
| `src/ui/Tooltip.jsx` | **New** (~30 lines) | 6 |
| `src/ui/Tooltip.module.css` | **New** (~40 lines) | 6 |
| `src/ui/Toolbar.jsx` | Wrap buttons in Tooltip | 6 |
| `src/ui/Modal.module.css` | Edit heading/button styles | 7 |

**CSS-only**: 9 files | **JSX**: 2 existing + 1 new | **HTML**: 1

---

## Verification

1. **Phase 1**: Load app — all colors/fonts update globally. Check blueprint dimensions still use JetBrains Mono.
2. **Phase 2**: Hover/click every toolbar button — verify lift, press, warm gold active glow. Save dot pulses gold.

Perfect! Here's a comprehensive breakdown of the original color scheme vs. the "Precision Atelier" redesign:

## Original Color Scheme → Precision Atelier Changes

### 1. **Primary Accent Color**
- **Original**: `--color-accent: #0D7C66` (a muted teal-green)
- **New**: `--color-accent: #1A7A68` (slightly lighter teal, kept for other uses)
- **NEW VAR**: `--color-active-glow: #D4A94C` (warm amber-gold for active/selected states)

The biggest change: accent colors shifted from **teal to amber-gold** for active states.

### 2. **Typography**
- **Original**: `--font-ui: 'Outfit', system-ui, -apple-system, sans-serif`
- **New**: `--font-ui: 'DM Sans', system-ui, -apple-system, sans-serif`
- **NEW**: `--font-heading: 'Plus Jakarta Sans', 'DM Sans', system-ui, sans-serif` (for labels)

### 3. **Active/Selected State Colors**
**Original** (used `--color-accent: #0D7C66`):
- Hover background: `rgba(13, 124, 102, 0.08)` → `var(--color-accent-subtle)`
- Active text: `var(--color-accent)` (the teal)

input.content:
# Fix: Kill the AI-Slop Color Palette

## Context

The previous "Precision Atelier" redesign introduced amber-gold (`#D4A94C`) active states — the classic move where AI tries to be "warm" by adding buttery gold to everything. At low opacity on cream backgrounds, these colors become washed-out, indecisive, and scream "generated." The structural changes (tooltips, micro-interactions, toast) are solid. The **colors** are the problem.

### What's wrong specifically
- `--color-active-glow: #D4A94C` — generic "warm gold," muddy on cream
- All `rgba(212, 169, 76, ...)` variants — invisible at 10-12% opacity on warm backgrounds
- The warm-on-warm combination (gold accent + cream surface) = zero contrast, everything blurs together
- DM Sans + Plus Jakarta Sans — functional but trending toward AI-overused territory

---

## Design Direction: "Architect's Ink"

**One rule: cool accent on warm surface.** This is how actual architectural drawings work — deep ink marks on warm vellum paper. The contrast between cool precision marks and warm paper is what makes blueprints visually compelling.

**Active state color: Prussian Ink `#2D5F8E`**
- Deep, saturated blue referencing architectural ink / cyanotype blueprints
- Creates natural warm/cool contrast against the cream `#F4F3F0` panels
- Clearly distinct from both teal accent (`#1A7A68`) and danger red (`#DC2626`)
- Professional CAD tools universally use blue for selection — it's domain-appropriate, not arbitrary
- At 10% opacity on cream, it produces a visible cool-tinted wash (unlike amber which disappeared)

**Font: Manrope**
- Geometric sans-serif with genuine character (distinctive 'a', 'g', numeral forms)
- Single font family for both UI and headings (weight variation for hierarchy)
- On Google Fonts, weights 400-700
- NOT in the AI-overused tier (unlike DM Sans, Inter, Plus Jakarta Sans)

---

## Changes

### 1. `index.html` — Font import
Replace DM Sans + Plus Jakarta Sans with Manrope:
```
family=Manrope:wght@400;500;600;700
```
Keep JetBrains Mono.

### 2. `src/styles/variables.css` — Design tokens
**Font vars:**
- `--font-ui: 'Manrope', system-ui, -apple-system, sans-serif`
- `--font-heading: 'Manrope', system-ui, -apple-system, sans-serif` (same family, just heavier weights — no visual break from consuming CSS)

**Active state vars (replace amber → ink blue):**
- `--color-active-glow: #2D5F8E`
- `--color-active-glow-subtle: rgba(45, 95, 142, 0.10)`
- `--color-active-glow-border: rgba(45, 95, 142, 0.25)`
- `--shadow-active-glow: 0 0 0 2px rgba(45, 95, 142, 0.18), 0 0 8px rgba(45, 95, 142, 0.08)`

### 3. `src/styles/global.css` — Keyframes
Update `@keyframes warmPulse` rgba values from `(212, 169, 76, ...)` → `(45, 95, 142, ...)`

### 4. `src/ui/Toolbar.module.css` — Hardcoded amber values
- `.saveBtn::after` — update `box-shadow` rgba values
- `@keyframes warmPulse` (local copy) — update rgba values
- `.toolPaletteBtnActive:hover` — `rgba(212, 169, 76, 0.18)` → `rgba(45, 95, 142, 0.15)`
- `.toggleBtn.toggleActive:hover` — same replacement

### 5. `src/ui/Sidebar.module.css` — Hardcoded amber values
- `.itemSelected:hover` — `rgba(212, 169, 76, 0.18)` → `rgba(45, 95, 142, 0.15)`

### 6. `src/renderers/SvgCanvas.module.css`
- `.toastProgress` — already uses `var(--color-active-glow)`, no change needed

### 7. No changes needed to:
- `PropertiesPanel.module.css` — uses only CSS vars, not hardcoded rgba
- `InputField.module.css` — uses only CSS vars
- `Modal.module.css` — uses only CSS vars
- `Tooltip.jsx` / `Tooltip.module.css` — no color refs
- `Toolbar.jsx` / `SvgCanvas.jsx` — no color refs

---

## Files Changed (6 files)

| File | Change |
|---|---|
| `index.html` | Swap font import to Manrope |
| `src/styles/variables.css` | 2 font vars + 4 active-state color vars |
| `src/styles/global.css` | 2 rgba values in warmPulse keyframe |
| `src/ui/Toolbar.module.css` | 6 hardcoded rgba values |
| `src/ui/Sidebar.module.css` | 1 hardcoded rgba value |

Everything else picks up the changes through CSS variables automatically.

---

## Verification

1. Load app — Manrope font renders everywhere. JetBrains Mono still on blueprint dimensions.
2. Select a tool — active state shows crisp blue highlight, clearly visible on cream background.
3. Toggle sidebar/grid/snap — active toggles show blue, not washed-out amber.
4. Click sidebar items — selected items have blue left-border + blue text on cool-tinted background.
5. Focus any input — blue focus ring, clearly visible.
6. Trigger undo — toast progress bar is blue.
7. Mark project dirty — save dot pulses blue, not gold.
8. Overall impression: warm cream surface + cool blue active marks = ink on vellum, not AI-slop.

# UI/UX Redesign: "Precision Atelier"

## Context
The current UI is functional but visually generic — flat buttons that all look the same, no micro-interactions, native browser tooltips, and a cold teal/gray palette. The goal is to make the interface feel like a **professional architect's studio**: warm, precise, and psychologically productive. Users should feel capable and focused when they open the app, not overwhelmed by a wall of identical gray icons.

**Design direction**: Warm neutrals, amber/gold active states (precision & craft), tactile button physics, distinctive typography, and purposeful micro-interactions. Not cold/clinical, not playful/toy-like — professional warmth.

---

## Phase 1: Foundation — Design Tokens & Typography (highest impact, pure CSS)

### `index.html`
- Replace font import: swap `Outfit` for `DM Sans` (400,500,600,700) + `Plus Jakarta Sans` (600,700). Keep `JetBrains Mono`.

### `src/styles/variables.css`
Typography:
- `--font-ui`: `'DM Sans'` (geometric, excellent readability, not AI-slop)
- Add `--font-heading`: `'Plus Jakarta Sans'` (distinctive for section headers)

Colors — warm neutral shift:
- `--color-text-primary`: `#1E2433` (deep navy-charcoal, warmer)
- `--color-text-secondary`: `#5B6275` (warmer gray)
- `--color-text-tertiary`: `#94989F` (warm mid-gray)
- `--color-panel-bg`: `#F4F3F0` (warm parchment)
- `--color-wall-fill`: `#2D3444` / `--color-wall-stroke`: `#1E2433` (warmer)
- `--color-accent`: `#1A7A68` (warmer teal) / `--color-accent-hover`: `#156B5A`
- `--color-accent-subtle`: `rgba(26,122,104,0.08)`
- `--color-grid-minor`: `#E6E4DE` / `--color-grid-major`: `#D0CDC5` (warm)

New variables for active/selection states (amber-gold):
- `--color-active-glow`: `#D4A94C`
- `--color-active-glow-subtle`: `rgba(212,169,76,0.12)`
- `--color-active-glow-border`: `rgba(212,169,76,0.30)`

New variables for micro-interactions:
- `--shadow-hover`: `0 2px 8px rgba(0,0,0,0.10), 0 0 0 1px rgba(0,0,0,0.04)`
- `--shadow-pressed`: `inset 0 1px 3px rgba(0,0,0,0.12), 0 0 0 1px rgba(0,0,0,0.04)`
- `--shadow-active-glow`: `0 0 0 2px rgba(212,169,76,0.20), 0 0 8px rgba(212,169,76,0.10)`
- `--duration-micro`: `0.08s`
- `--ease-tactile`: `cubic-bezier(0.25,0.46,0.45,0.94)`

---

## Phase 2: Button Micro-interactions & Active Tool States (CSS-only)

### `src/ui/Toolbar.module.css`

**`.btn`** — add tactile feel:
- Add `box-shadow: inset 0 -1px 0 rgba(0,0,0,0.04)` (subtle bottom edge)
- Hover: `translateY(-1px)` + `var(--shadow-hover)` (lift)
- Active: `scale(0.97)` + `var(--shadow-pressed)` (press-in)
- Expand transition to include `transform` and `box-shadow`

**`.toolPaletteBtn`** — same lift/press treatment:
- Hover: `translateY(-1px)` + `var(--shadow-sm)`
- Active press: `scale(0.96)` + `var(--shadow-pressed)`

**`.toolPaletteBtnActive`** — warm gold glow (the "I'm using this tool" state):
- `background: var(--color-active-glow-subtle)`
- `color: var(--color-active-glow)`
- `border-color: var(--color-active-glow-border)`
- `box-shadow: var(--shadow-active-glow)`

content.text:
The core problem: amber-gold at low opacity on cream = invisible, muddy, AI-generated warmth. The fix is a **cool/warm contrast** — Prussian ink blue (`#2D5F8E`) active states on warm cream surfaces. This is how actual architectural drawings work: deep ink marks on vellum paper. The contrast is natural and intentional, not algorithmic.

Also swapping DM Sans + Plus Jakarta Sans (both trending AI-overused) for **Manrope** — a single geometric family with genuine character.

Only 5 files need edits. Most consuming CSS already uses `var(--color-active-glow)` etc., so changing the variable definitions in `variables.css` cascades everywhere automatically.

input.plan:
# Fix: Kill the AI-Slop Color Palette

## Context

The previous "Precision Atelier" redesign introduced amber-gold (`#D4A94C`) active states — the classic move where AI tries to be "warm" by adding buttery gold to everything. At low opacity on cream backgrounds, these colors become washed-out, indecisive, and scream "generated." The structural changes (tooltips, micro-interactions, toast) are solid. The **colors** are the problem.

### What's wrong specifically
- `--color-active-glow: #D4A94C` — generic "warm gold," muddy on cream
- All `rgba(212, 169, 76, ...)` variants — invisible at 10-12% opacity on warm backgrounds
- The warm-on-warm combination (gold accent + cream surface) = zero contrast, everything blurs together
- DM Sans + Plus Jakarta Sans — functional but trending toward AI-overused territory

---

## Design Direction: "Architect's Ink"

**One rule: cool accent on warm surface.** This is how actual architectural drawings work — deep ink marks on warm vellum paper. The contrast between cool precision marks and warm paper is what makes blueprints visually compelling.

**Active state color: Prussian Ink `#2D5F8E`**
- Deep, saturated blue referencing architectural ink / cyanotype blueprints
- Creates natural warm/cool contrast against the cream `#F4F3F0` panels
- Clearly distinct from both teal accent (`#1A7A68`) and danger red (`#DC2626`)
- Professional CAD tools universally use blue for selection — it's domain-appropriate, not arbitrary
- At 10% opacity on cream, it produces a visible cool-tinted wash (unlike amber which disappeared)

**Font: Manrope**
- Geometric sans-serif with genuine character (distinctive 'a', 'g', numeral forms)
- Single font family for both UI and headings (weight variation for hierarchy)
- On Google Fonts, weights 400-700
- NOT in the AI-overused tier (unlike DM Sans, Inter, Plus Jakarta Sans)

---

## Changes

### 1. `index.html` — Font import
Replace DM Sans + Plus Jakarta Sans with Manrope:
```
family=Manrope:wght@400;500;600;700
```
Keep JetBrains Mono.

### 2. `src/styles/variables.css` — Design tokens
**Font vars:**
- `--font-ui: 'Manrope', system-ui, -apple-system, sans-serif`
- `--font-heading: 'Manrope', system-ui, -apple-system, sans-serif` (same family, just heavier weights — no visual break from consuming CSS)

**Active state vars (replace amber → ink blue):**
- `--color-active-glow: #2D5F8E`
- `--color-active-glow-subtle: rgba(45, 95, 142, 0.10)`
- `--color-active-glow-border: rgba(45, 95, 142, 0.25)`
- `--shadow-active-glow: 0 0 0 2px rgba(45, 95, 142, 0.18), 0 0 8px rgba(45, 95, 142, 0.08)`

### 3. `src/styles/global.css` — Keyframes
Update `@keyframes warmPulse` rgba values from `(212, 169, 76, ...)` → `(45, 95, 142, ...)`

### 4. `src/ui/Toolbar.module.css` — Hardcoded amber values
- `.saveBtn::after` — update `box-shadow` rgba values
- `@keyframes warmPulse` (local copy) — update rgba values
- `.toolPaletteBtnActive:hover` — `rgba(212, 169, 76, 0.18)` → `rgba(45, 95, 142, 0.15)`
- `.toggleBtn.toggleActive:hover` — same replacement

### 5. `src/ui/Sidebar.module.css` — Hardcoded amber values
- `.itemSelected:hover` — `rgba(212, 169, 76, 0.18)` → `rgba(45, 95, 142, 0.15)`

### 6. `src/renderers/SvgCanvas.module.css`
- `.toastProgress` — already uses `var(--color-active-glow)`, no change needed

### 7. No changes needed to:
- `PropertiesPanel.module.css` — uses only CSS vars, not hardcoded rgba
- `InputField.module.css` — uses only CSS vars
- `Modal.module.css` — uses only CSS vars
- `Tooltip.jsx` / `Tooltip.module.css` — no color refs
- `Toolbar.jsx` / `SvgCanvas.jsx` — no color refs

---

## Files Changed (6 files)

| File | Change |
|---|---|
| `index.html` | Swap font import to Manrope |
| `src/styles/variables.css` | 2 font vars + 4 active-state color vars |
| `src/styles/global.css` | 2 rgba values in warmPulse keyframe |
| `src/ui/Toolbar.module.css` | 6 hardcoded rgba values |
| `src/ui/Sidebar.module.css` | 1 hardcoded rgba value |

Everything else picks up the changes through CSS variables automatically.

---

## Verification

1. Load app — Manrope font renders everywhere. JetBrains Mono still on blueprint dimensions.
2. Select a tool — active state shows crisp blue highlight, clearly visible on cream background.
3. Toggle sidebar/grid/snap — active toggles show blue, not washed-out amber.
4. Click sidebar items — selected items have blue left-border + blue text on cool-tinted background.
5. Focus any input — blue focus ring, clearly visible.
6. Trigger undo — toast progress bar is blue.
7. Mark project dirty — save dot pulses blue, not gold.
8. Overall impression: warm cream surface + cool blue active marks = ink on vellum, not AI-slop.
